page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs, utilizing AMD EPYC 7H12 processors operating at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each vCPU maps directly to a physical core, ensuring dedicated compute allocation without hyperthreading resource sharing.

The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning storage needs must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps, facilitating reliable data transfer rates for distributed environments.

This hardware configuration is designed for workloads requiring consistent, non-shared CPU performance and a balanced memory profile. It is well-suited for enterprise applications, web serving, medium-sized databases, and containerized microservices that benefit from dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources without requiring local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7H12 processors running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, it provides 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on external storage solutions, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. This resource profile makes it suitable for enterprise applications, web serving, and containerized workloads.

tagline: A dedicated sixty-four core AMD EPYC compute instance with a balanced memory profile and sixteen gigabits per second baseline network bandwidth.
